name: Fall of Avdiivka
target: City of Avdiivka, Donetsk Oblast
casualties: Russia: ~47,186 troops (17,000 killed, 30,000 wounded), 364 tanks, 748 AFVs lost. Ukraine: 850-1,000 POWs captured during withdrawal.
description: After a 4-month siege beginning October 2023, Russian forces captured Avdiivka following Ukraine's withdrawal on Feb 17. One of the bloodiest battles of the war, with Russian daily casualties reaching 983 killed/wounded — the highest rate since the invasion began.

name: Sinking of Landing Ship Caesar Kunikov
target: Ropucha-class landing ship Caesar Kunikov
description: Ukraine's military intelligence unit Group 13 sank the Russian landing ship Caesar Kunikov using Magura V5 sea drones 2.5 miles off Alupka, southern Crimea. The strike disabled a third of Russia's Black Sea Fleet landing capability.

name: Sinking of Patrol Ship Sergei Kotov
target: Project 22160 patrol ship Sergei Kotov
casualties: Ship sunk near Kerch Strait; crew casualties unknown
description: Ukrainian Magura V5 maritime drones struck and sank the 1,300-ton Russian patrol ship Sergei Kotov near the Kerch Strait, hitting the stern, starboard and port sides and sparking a fire that destroyed the vessel.

name: Zaporizhzhia Nuclear Plant Drone Strike
target: Zaporizhzhia Nuclear Power Plant, Unit 6 reactor dome
casualties: No casualties reported; no nuclear safety compromise
description: Drones struck the Zaporizhzhia nuclear complex with a direct hit on the reactor dome of Unit 6 — the first direct targeting of the plant since November 2022. IAEA Director-General Grossi condemned the attack as a 'dangerous new stage' in the conflict.

name: Russian Kharkiv Border Offensive (Vovchansk)
target: Vovchansk and Lyptsi, Kharkiv Oblast border zone
description: Russia launched a cross-border armored assault with 4-5 infantry battalions into northern Kharkiv Oblast at 05:00, opening a new front aimed at creating a buffer zone. Forces entered Vovchansk and advanced to the river Vovcha, but the offensive stalled by early June after Ukraine stabilized the front.

name: Destruction of Missile Corvette Tsiklon in Sevastopol
target: Project 22800 Karakurt-class missile corvette Tsiklon
description: Ukrainian forces destroyed the Tsiklon missile corvette in the port of Sevastopol using ATACMS missiles. Ukraine claimed this strike effectively eliminated all Black Sea Fleet missile carrier capability based in Crimea.

name: Burgenstock Ukraine Peace Summit
target: Peace framework negotiations
description: Switzerland hosted a global peace summit at Burgenstock Resort attended by 92 nations and 57 heads of state. A Joint Communique signed by 84 states affirmed Ukraine's territorial integrity as a precondition for peace. Russia and China did not attend; key Global South nations declined to sign.

name: EU 14th Sanctions Package — First LNG Restrictions
target: Russian LNG industry, transshipment, circumvention networks
description: The EU enacted its 14th sanctions package, the first to target Russia's LNG industry, prohibiting EU-based reloading services for Russian LNG transshipment, banning imports through unconnected terminals, and blocking investment in Arctic LNG 2 and Murmansk LNG projects. Total Russian designations exceeded 2,200.

name: Sevastopol Beach ATACMS Strike
target: Russian missile launcher near Sevastopol (per US official); submunitions scattered over beach
description: Five ATACMS missiles armed with cluster submunitions were fired at Sevastopol. Russian air defense intercepted four but caused the fifth to deviate, scattering submunitions over beaches at Uchkuyivka and Lyubimovka. Russia blamed the US; a US official confirmed the target was a Russian missile launcher.

name: Okhmatdyt Children's Hospital Missile Strike
target: Okhmatdyt Children's Hospital, Kyiv — Ukraine's largest pediatric facility
casualties: 2 medical workers killed, at least 8 children injured; 627 patients inside during attack
description: A Russian Kh-101 cruise missile struck the toxicology department of Okhmatdyt hospital during a 36-missile barrage, while children were undergoing hemodialysis. The UN confirmed 'high likelihood' of a direct hit, contradicting Russia's claim that Ukrainian air defense caused the damage. Investigated as a war crime.

name: Ukraine's Kursk Oblast Incursion
target: Kursk Oblast, Russia — border zone settlements
description: Ukraine launched a cross-border incursion into Russia's Kursk Oblast at 08:00 MSK, preceded by overnight artillery and drone strikes. By week's end, Ukraine claimed 1,000 sq km of Russian territory and 28 settlements captured. A military administration was established on August 15.

name: Zaporizhzhia Nuclear Plant Cooling Tower Fire
target: Zaporizhzhia Nuclear Power Plant cooling tower
casualties: No casualties; no immediate nuclear safety threat
description: IAEA observers at the Zaporizhzhia plant reported an alleged drone attack on one of the cooling towers, with strong dark smoke observed rising from the structure. The IAEA confirmed no immediate nuclear safety threat but noted it as part of escalating military activity near the plant.

name: Largest Russian Aerial Attack of the War
target: Energy infrastructure across 15+ regions — Kyiv, Kharkiv, Dnipro, Odesa, Zaporizhzhia, and others
description: Russia carried out its largest aerial attack since the 2022 invasion: 127 missiles and 109 drones over eight hours, including Kinzhal hypersonic missiles. Blackouts hit Kyiv, Volyn, Rivne, Odesa, and Chernihiv regions. The attack lasted over eight hours and targeted at least 15 regions.

name: Poltava Military Institute Strike
target: Military Institute of Telecommunications and Information Technologies, Poltava
casualties: 55 killed, 328 injured — one of the deadliest single strikes of the war
description: Two Russian missiles struck a branch of the Poltava Military Institute of Communications and a nearby hospital, hitting as personnel were moving to bomb shelters after the air raid alert. Part of the institute building was destroyed. Zelensky ordered an investigation and demanded more Western air defense systems.

name: North Korean Troop Deployment to Kursk
target: Reinforcement of Russian forces in Kursk Oblast against Ukrainian incursion
casualties: By Jan 2025: South Korean intel estimated 300 DPRK killed, 2,700 wounded
description: Approximately 10,000-12,000 North Korean soldiers in five infantry brigades arrived in Kursk Oblast, confirmed by NATO, the US DoD, and later by North Korea itself (April 2025). Forces were trained in eastern Russia before deployment and led by Colonel General Kim Yong Bok, commander of DPRK special forces.

name: November 17 Massive Energy Grid Strike
target: Ukrainian energy grid — generation and distribution across multiple oblasts
description: Russia launched 120 missiles and 90 drones in its largest air strike in nearly three months, causing severe damage to the energy system. Ukraine intercepted 102 missiles and 42 drones. Power cuts hit regions from Lviv in the west to Zaporizhzhia in the southeast, marking the beginning of winter energy warfare.

name: Biden Authorizes ATACMS Strikes on Russian Territory
target: Policy shift: authorization for Ukraine to use US-supplied ATACMS against targets inside Russia
description: President Biden reversed longstanding US policy and authorized Ukraine to use ATACMS missiles (range: 190 miles) against targets inside Russia, primarily in response to North Korea's deployment of troops to Kursk. Initially limited to Kursk Oblast defensive positions and Russian logistics targets.

name: First Ukrainian ATACMS Strike on Russian Soil
target: Munitions storage facility near Karachev, Bryansk Oblast
description: At 03:25 Moscow time, Ukraine fired six ATACMS ballistic missiles at a munitions depot near Karachev in Bryansk Oblast, over 70 miles inside Russia. This was the first confirmed use of US-supplied ATACMS on Russian territory, just two days after Biden lifted restrictions.

name: First Storm Shadow Strike on Russian Territory
target: Military command post near Maryino, Kursk Oblast (believed to house Russian and North Korean command staff)
description: Ukraine fired up to 12 UK-supplied Storm Shadow cruise missiles at a military facility near Maryino in Kursk Oblast, the first confirmed use of these weapons on Russian soil. The 450-kg BROACH warheads are designed to penetrate fortified underground bunkers. Missile fragments were recovered from the site.

name: First Use of Oreshnik Hypersonic Missile
target: PA Pivdenmash (Southern Machine-Building Plant), Dnipro
casualties: Unknown; warheads carried 'dummy' submunitions with limited explosive payload
description: Russia debuted the Oreshnik intermediate-range hypersonic missile, launched from Kapustin Yar in Astrakhan Oblast, striking Dnipro. The nuclear-capable missile carried six MIRV warheads — a technology previously exclusive to nuclear weapons. Putin stated it was retaliation for Western long-range missile authorization.

name: November 28 Infrastructure Barrage — 1M+ Homes Without Power
target: Energy infrastructure including western Ukraine regions previously less affected
description: Russia fired 91 missiles and 97 attack drones targeting critical energy infrastructure across Ukraine, including its westernmost regions for the first time at significant scale. Ukraine intercepted 79 missiles and 35 drones. Over one million homes lost power. This was the 11th massive Russian attack on civilian energy infrastructure in 2024.

name: Christmas Day Energy Grid Attack
target: Thermal power plants, boiler houses, and electricity infrastructure — Kharkiv, Dnipro, Poltava regions
casualties: 1 killed; widespread blackouts across multiple regions
description: Russia launched 78 missiles and 106 Shahed drones on Christmas morning targeting fuel and energy infrastructure. DTEK's thermal power plant was struck for the 13th time in 2024. Twelve missiles targeted Kharkiv alone. Biden condemned the attack as 'outrageous' and vowed a weapons surge to Ukraine.

name: Russian Drone Strike on IAEA Vehicle Near Zaporizhzhia
target: Clearly marked IAEA vehicle transporting observers to Zaporizhzhia NPP for rotation
description: A Russian drone struck a clearly marked IAEA vehicle 8 km from the front line on the Ukrainian side while it was driving an observer team to the Zaporizhzhia nuclear plant for rotation. The attack raised serious concerns about respect for international organizations operating near the conflict zone.

name: End of Russian Gas Transit Through Ukraine to Europe
target: Russian natural gas pipeline transit through Ukraine
description: At 08:00 on January 1, Russian gas flow through Ukraine to Europe was shut off after the 2019 five-year transit contract expired. Ukraine refused renewal 'in the interest of national security,' ending a 60-year gas transit era. Ukraine loses ~$450M/year in fees; Slovakia and Austria switched to alternative routes smoothly.

name: Russian Sumy Oblast Cross-Border Offensive
target: Sumy Oblast border zone — Basivka, Novenke, villages toward Sumy city
description: Russia launched a cross-border offensive from Kursk into Sumy Oblast, succeeding the Kursk recapture campaign. The 810th Brigade entered Basivka and advanced toward multiple villages. By June, Russian forces were within 20 km of Sumy city. Putin announced the objective was a 'security buffer zone'; Zelensky reported 50,000 Russian troops amassed.

name: Largest Drone Attack on Moscow
target: Moscow and Moscow Oblast
description: Ukraine launched what Russian authorities described as the largest ever drone attack on the Russian capital. The Russian Defense Ministry said it downed 337 Ukrainian drones, 91 of which fell in the Moscow region. Air and rail travel faced massive disruptions, and three major airports diverted flights.

name: Ukraine Agrees to 30-Day Partial Ceasefire
target: Energy infrastructure mutual ceasefire
description: Ukraine agreed to a 30-day partial ceasefire following US pressure, after Washington suspended all military aid and intelligence sharing with Ukraine. The ceasefire was limited in scope and Russia continued attacks despite diplomatic pressure.

name: Russia Claims Full Recapture of Kursk Oblast
target: Ukrainian-held territory in Kursk Oblast
casualties: DPRK: 300 killed, 2,700 wounded (South Korean intel, Jan 2025)
description: Russian Chief of Staff Gerasimov declared all territory in Kursk 'liberated' after recapturing 50-60% through a months-long counteroffensive aided by 10,000+ North Korean troops. Ukraine immediately disputed the claim, saying its forces remained operational in parts of Kursk Oblast.

name: First Direct Russia-Ukraine Talks Since 2022 (Istanbul)
target: Ceasefire negotiations and prisoner exchange
description: Ukrainian and Russian delegations held direct talks in Istanbul for the first time in three years. Talks lasted less than two hours with no ceasefire agreement — Russia demanded Ukraine withdraw from four oblasts it claims to have annexed. The sole achievement was agreement on a 1,000-for-1,000 prisoner exchange.

name: Second Round Istanbul Talks
target: Peace framework memorandums exchanged
description: Both sides presented formal memorandums outlining their peace proposals in a second round of talks. No breakthrough was achieved, but both committed to continue dialogue and confirmed prisoner exchange arrangements. Drone attacks on Ukraine continued even during the talks.

name: Operation Spiderweb — Covert Strike on Russian Air Bases
target: Five Russian air bases: Belaya, Dyagilevo, Ivanovo Severny, Olenya, and Ukrainka
casualties: 10-20 aircraft destroyed (US officials); Ukraine claims 41 destroyed/disabled, ~$7B in losses
description: The SBU executed a covert drone attack using 117 Ukrainian-made Osa quadcopters concealed in wooden containers on flatbed trucks driven into Russia. The drones were launched simultaneously at five strategic bomber bases. 18 months in planning, the US was not informed in advance. It was the largest drone attack on Russian air bases in the war.

name: EU 18th Sanctions Package — Refined Russian Oil Products Ban
target: Russian petroleum products refined from Russian crude in third-party countries
description: The EU adopted its 18th sanctions package, closing a major loophole by banning imports of petroleum products refined from Russian crude oil in third-party countries. This targeted the practice of routing Russian oil through intermediary refiners to bypass existing sanctions.

name: Russian Claimed Capture of Chasiv Yar
target: City of Chasiv Yar, Donetsk Oblast — elevated fortified position
casualties: Russia: est. 4,880 casualties (1,967 killed, 2,897 wounded, 16 captured) from Apr 2024-Feb 2025 alone
description: After 16 months of grinding urban combat beginning April 2024, Russia's MoD claimed capture of Chasiv Yar with 4,200+ buildings cleared. Ukraine denied full capture, showing forces at the western edge. The city's elevated position would give Russia fire control over supply routes toward Kramatorsk and Sloviansk.

name: Fall of Pokrovsk to Russian Forces
target: Pokrovsk and Myrnohrad, western Donetsk Oblast — key supply hub
casualties: Russia lost 1,000+ armored vehicles and 500+ tanks in the Pokrovsk area since Oct 2023; daily attacks of 30-60
description: Russian forces entered Pokrovsk in November 2025 after a prolonged siege with an average advance rate of 70 meters/day — slower than the Battle of the Somme. CSIS assessed that despite capturing the supply hub, Russia was unable to make operationally significant advances west of Pokrovsk by February 2026.

name: Intensified Strikes on Odesa Port Infrastructure
target: Pivdennyi port, Chornomorsk port, Allseeds shipping terminal, grain storage, oil tanks
casualties: At least 1 killed at Allseeds terminal; 8 killed, 30+ wounded in Pivdennyi ballistic strike
description: Russia escalated near-daily drone and missile attacks on Odesa Oblast ports throughout December, hitting Ukraine's largest port (Pivdennyi), destroying thousands of tonnes of sunflower oil storage, and reducing one of three key export ports to 20% capacity. The campaign caused a 37% drop in grain exports year-over-year.

name: EU Extends Economic Sanctions on Russia for 6 Months
target: Comprehensive economic sanctions regime
description: The EU Council extended its comprehensive economic sanctions against Russia for another six months, maintaining all existing restrictions on trade, finance, energy, and individuals. Despite sanctions, Russia earned $235 billion from oil and gas exports in 2024, 0.5% more than 2023.

name: Zaporizhzhia NPP Loses All Off-Site Power (12th Time)
target: Zaporizhzhia Nuclear Power Plant external power supply
casualties: No casualties; nuclear safety risk elevated
description: Widespread military activity disrupted Ukraine's electrical grid, causing the Zaporizhzhia nuclear plant to temporarily lose all off-site power for the 12th time since the conflict began. The IAEA described the plant's condition as 'extremely fragile,' relying on a single off-site power line.

name: Trump-Zelensky Mar-a-Lago Summit — '95% Agreed' Framework
target: 20-point peace framework and security guarantees
description: Trump and Zelensky met at Mar-a-Lago and announced the 20-point framework agreement was '95% agreed' with security guarantees '100% agreed.' The US offered 15-year security guarantees similar to NATO-level assurances. Zelensky said he was prepared to drop Ukraine's NATO membership bid. Territory remains the thorniest issue.

name: Paris Peace Talks — UK/France Troop Commitment Declaration
target: Peacekeeping framework and security architecture for Ukraine
description: US, European, and Ukrainian representatives gathered in Paris for talks described by American officials as a 'real milestone.' The UK and France signed a Declaration of Intent to deploy troops to Ukraine to safeguard any future peace deal. Russia rejected any NATO troops on Ukrainian soil.

name: Second Oreshnik Hypersonic Missile Strike — Lviv Region
target: Critical infrastructure facility in Lviv Oblast, near Polish/NATO border
casualties: At least 4 killed in broader barrage
description: Russia used the Oreshnik intermediate-range hypersonic missile for the second time, striking western Ukraine near the Polish border at 13,000 km/h. Russia claimed it was retaliation for an alleged Ukrainian drone strike on a Putin residence. Analysts assessed the strike as primarily psychological signaling to NATO, targeting the EU/NATO frontier.

name: Ukrainian Deep-Strike Campaign Against Russian Refineries
target: Russian oil refineries — 61+ strikes since January 2024, including Samara region
casualties: Unknown military; Russian civilian gas prices increased
description: Ukraine's sustained drone campaign against Russian refineries reduced refining capacity by approximately 17% (1.1 million bbl/day per Reuters). By March 2026, over 7,000 drone systems were launched monthly, some reaching 1,500 km into Russia. Ukraine's sanctions commissioner called it 'long-range sanctions' targeting Russia's biggest financial lifeline.

name: Ukrainian Drone Strikes on Baltic Oil Export Terminals
parties: Ukraine (SBU, Unmanned Systems Forces, SOF, GUR, Border Guard)
target: Ust-Luga and Primorsk oil export terminals on the Baltic Sea — handling 2M bbl/day of Russian crude
description: Ukraine launched its largest drone attack of the year against Russia's Baltic oil export ports, striking Ust-Luga and Primorsk four times in one week from 935 km away. Zelensky stated 60% of Ust-Luga's capacity was knocked out. Reuters reported the campaign may have taken 40% of Russia's overall export capacity offline — the most severe oil disruption in modern Russian history.

name: EU 20th Sanctions Package — Crypto, Energy, Military-Industrial
target: Russian energy revenues, military-industrial complex, crypto assets, financial services
description: The EU's 20th sanctions package — its largest listings package in two years with 120 new individual designations — targeted energy, financial services, trade, and for the first time cryptocurrency transactions. A full transaction ban on Nord Stream 1 and 2 was imposed, preventing any future use of the pipelines.

name: US Treasury Sanctions Major Russian Oil Companies
target: Major Russian oil companies; Trump threatened further sanctions if Russia rejected 30-day ceasefire
description: Trump warned that the US would impose further sanctions on Russia if it did not agree to a 30-day unconditional ceasefire. The US Treasury sanctioned major Russian oil companies as part of pressure to bring Moscow to the negotiating table. Russia rejected the ceasefire call and continued military operations.

name: Ukrainian Withdrawal from Krynky Bridgehead
target: Krynky bridgehead on the left (east) bank of the Dnipro River, Kherson Oblast
casualties: Heavy Ukrainian Marine casualties during months-long operation
description: Ukraine was forced to withdraw from its Krynky bridgehead on the east bank of the Dnipro River after months of grueling combat. The bridgehead, established during Ukraine's 2023 counter-offensive, had been maintained at enormous cost in personnel. Russia subsequently contested the Dnipro islands, assembling 300+ boats for potential crossing operations.
